patch_description: A completely different algorithmic approach: a simulated-annealing (SA) search over circle centers with a soft feasibility objective, coupled with a fast feasibility projection to radii. Instead of optimizing radii directly for fixed centers (as in the LP-on-centers approach), this method explores centers to maximize the achievable total radius after projecting radii to the feasible region. At the end, a rigorous linear-programming projection is applied to obtain the maximal feasible radii for the chosen centers. This “centers-first SA with LP projection” strategy explores disparate layouts (including restarts from a deterministic seed) and tends to find higher total radii than purely center-fixed LP methods, especially in larger-n circle packings. The final radii are guaranteed feasible by the LP projection.
